title: LM Studio Cheat Sheet
description: Essential commands and techniques for using LM Studio effectively.

commands.description: Sets the randomness of the model's output.
commands.scenario: When building a code review bot that needs deterministic output.

commands.description: Specifies the model and enables streaming responses.
commands.scenario: When you need real-time feedback during a live demo.

commands.command: "Think step by step. Then verify your answer."
commands.description: Encourages the model to reason through problems methodically.
commands.scenario: When solving complex engineering problems that require logical steps.

commands.description: Limits the response length to control verbosity and cost.
commands.scenario: When generating concise summaries or answers.

commands.description: Enables detailed logging for debugging purposes.
commands.scenario: When troubleshooting issues with API requests or responses.

commands.description: Instructs the model to format the output in bullet points.
commands.scenario: When generating lists or structured information.

commands.description: Defines custom stop sequences to end the model's output.
commands.scenario: When you need to terminate output after specific markers.

commands.description: Sets a timeout for requests to prevent hanging.
commands.scenario: When integrating with systems that require quick responses.

commands.description: Prompts the model to simplify explanations.
commands.scenario: When explaining complex technical concepts to non-experts.

commands.description: Reduces repetition in the model's output.
commands.scenario: When generating creative content like stories or poems.

commands.description: Simulates the command without executing it.
commands.scenario: When testing configurations or scripts safely.

commands.description: Requests the model to give an in-depth analysis.
commands.scenario: When performing technical reviews or deep dives.

commands.description: Nucleus sampling parameter to control diversity by selecting tokens from the smallest set whose cumulative probability exceeds the threshold.

commands.command: system_prompt="You are a Senior DevOps Engineer."
commands.description: Defining a persistent system role to anchor the model's persona and behavioral constraints across a session.
Offloads a specific number of layers to the GPU to accelerate inference when VRAM allows.
Penalizes new tokens based on whether they have appeared in the text so far, encouraging the model to discuss new topics.
Explicitly sets the KV cache size to manage memory usage and prevent out-of-memory errors during long conversations.
Sets a specific seed to ensure deterministic outputs for testing and debugging model consistency.
Specifies the duration (in seconds) to keep the model loaded in memory after the last request to reduce cold-start latency.
Adjusts the penalty applied to tokens that have already appeared, preventing the model from entering repetitive loops.
Used to manually decrease or increase the probability of specific tokens appearing in the output (e.g., banning specific words).
Providing 2-3 examples of input/output pairs within the prompt to guide the model's response format and style.
